name: Pang Lin
page_title: Pang Lin 龐林 [Shu, Wei] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
historical_notes:
Pang Lin was Pang Tong’s brother, also in service to Shu. He held rank as Zhi Zhong of Jingzhou. With Huang Quan, he participated in the Yiling campaign, and after defeat, travelled with Huang Quan into Wei, where they both surrendered. He was titled Li Hou, and later made Governor of Julu.

Pang Lin’s wife hailed from the same prefecture as as Xi Jing’s sister. When the Cao family conquered Jingzhou, Pang Lin was separated from his wife, who took care of their daughter alone. After he surrendered to Wei with Huang Quan, he was also reunited with his wife and daughter. When Cao Pi learned of this, he praised Pang Lin for being a loyal husband, and sent him many gifts.

sections.short-biography: Surrendered to Wei with Huang Quan, where Lin was reunited with his wife.

full_text:
Pang Lin 龐林
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Pang Lin (龐林)was born in Nan. Surrendered to Wei with Huang Quan, where Lin was reunited with his wife. Pang Lin was affiliated with the Shu Kingdom and the Wei Kingdom.

BiographyHistoric (Confirmed)
Pang Lin was Pang Tong’s brother, also in service to Shu. He held rank as Zhi Zhong of Jingzhou. With Huang Quan, he participated in the Yiling campaign, and after defeat, travelled with Huang Quan into Wei, where they both surrendered. He was titled Li Hou, and later made Governor of Julu.
Pang Lin’s wife hailed from the same prefecture as as Xi Jing’s sister. When the Cao family conquered Jingzhou, Pang Lin was separated from his wife, who took care of their daughter alone. After he surrendered to Wei with Huang Quan, he was also reunited with his wife and daughter. When Cao Pi learned of this, he praised Pang Lin for being a loyal husband, and sent him many gifts.
